question: Find the value of y, if log (y + 8) + log (y - 8) = 2log 3 + 2log 5
solution:
log (y + 8) + log (y - 8) = 2log 3 + 2log 5

⇒ log (y + 8)(y - 8) = 2log 3 + 2log 5 (log ab = log a + log b)

⇒ log (y<sup>2</sup> -8y + 8y - 64) = log 3<sup>2</sup> + log 5<sup>2</sup>


⇒ log (y<sup>2</sup> - 64) = log 3<sup>2</sup> + log 5<sup>2</sup>


⇒ log (y<sup>2</sup> - 64) = log 9 + log 25

⇒ log (y<sup>2</sup> - 64) = log (9 × 25)

⇒ log(y<sup>2</sup> - 64) = log 225

⇒ y<sup>2</sup> - 64 = 225

⇒ y<sup>2</sup> = 225 + 64

⇒ y<sup>2</sup> = 289

⇒ y = ±√289

∴ y = ±17

question: The interior angle of a regular polygon is five times the size of its exterior angle. Identify the polygon.
solution:
An interior angle of a regular polygon = ((2n−4)×90)/n


An exterior angle of a regular polygon = 360/n


((2n−4)×90)/n = 5 ×  360/n (Given)
= (2n-4) x 90 = 5 x 360
= 180n - 360 = 1800
= 180n = 1800 + 360
= 180n = 2160
= n = 2160/180 = 12
The polygon has 12 sides which is dodecagon

question: Find the area, to the nearest cm<sup>2</sup>, of the triangle whose sides are in the ratio 2 : 3 : 4 and whose perimeter is 180 cm.
solution:
Let the length of the sides of triangle be 2x, 3x and 4x.

Perimeter of triangle = 180cm

⇒ 2x + 3x + 4x = 180
⇒ 9x = 180
⇒ x=180/9
 = 20 cm

Then the sides of the triangle are:

2x = 2 × 20 = 40 cm; 3x = 3 × 20 = 60cm and 4x = 4 × 20 = 80cm

Using Heron's formula

Area of triangle = s√(s−a)(s−b)(s−c)
Where s = (a + b + c)/2
Let a = 40cm, b = 60cm, c = 80cm and s = (40 + 60 + 80)/2 =180/2 = 90cm

⇒ A = √90(90−40)(90−60)(90−80)=√90×50×30×10=√(1350000)
∴ A =1162cm<sup>2</sup> (to the nearest cm<sup>2</sup>)

question:
The area A of a circle is increasing at a constant rate of 1.5 cm <sup>2</sup>s<sup>−1</sup>
. Find, to 3 significant figures, the rate at which the radius r of the circle is increasing when the area of the circle is 2 cm<sup>2</sup>
.
solution:
Area of a circle (A) = πr<sup>2</sup>


Given

dA/dt=1.5 cm<sup>2</sup>s<sup>-1</sup>


dr/dt = ?

A = 2cm<sup>2</sup>


Now

2 = πr <sup>2</sup> 


= r<sup>2</sup> = 2/π


r = √(2/π) cm = 0.798cm

dr/dt = dA/dt × dr/dt


dA/dr= 2πr (differentiating A = πr<sup>2</sup>)


dr/dA=1/2πr


dr/dt=1.5 × 1/(2 × π × 0.798) = 1.5 × 0.199


dr/dt = 0.299cms<sup>−1</sup> (to 3 s.f)

question: What is the general term of the sequence 3, 8, 13, 18, ...?
solution:
Given the sequence 3, 8, 13, 18, ... which is an arithmetic sequence

a = 3

d = T<sub>2</sub>  - T<sub>1</sub>  = 8 - 3 = 5

The general term of an A.P is:

T<sub>n</sub>  = a + (n - 1)d

⇒ T<sub>n</sub> = 3 + (n - 1)5

= T<sub>n</sub> = 3 + 5n - 5

∴ T<sub>n</sub> = 5n - 2

question: The population of a village decreased from 1,230 to 1,040 due to breakout of an epidemic. What is the percentage decrease in the population?
solution:
Original population = 1,230
New population = 1,040
Decrease in population = 1,230 – 1,040 = 190
Percentage decrease in population =(decrease in population/original population) x 100%         

= 190/1,230
 x 100 = 15.45%

question: Find the compound interest (CI) on ₦15,700 for 2 years at 8% per annum compounded annually.
How many students scored at least 25%
solution: Number of students who scored atleast 25% = 5 + 3 + 8 = 16

question: Find the value of y if 402<sub>y</sub> =102<sub>ten</sub>
Solve for x: 3(x – 1) ≤ 2 (x – 3)
solution:
3(x – 1) ≤ 2 (x – 3)

= 3x – 3 ≤ 2x – 6

Add 3 to both sides

= 3x – 3+ 3 ≤ 2x – 6+ 3

= 3x ≤ 2x – 3

Subtract 2x from both the sides

= 3x – 2x ≤ 2x – 3 – 2x

∴ x ≤ -3

question: The ages of students in a small primary school were recorded in the table below.                                                                               Estimate the median.
solution:
(N + 1)/2  = (107 + 1)/2 th = 54th value i.e the median age is in the interval "7 - 8" (29 + 25 = 54)?

Median = 1<sub>m</sub> + (((∑f)/2 − f<sub>b</sub>) /f<sub>m</sub> )c

l<sub>m</sub>= lower class boundary of the median age = 6.5

∑f = 107

f<sub>b</sub> = sum of all frequencies before the median age = 29

f<sub>m</sub> = frequency of the median age = 40

c = class width = 8.5 - 6.5 = 2

Median = 6.5 + (((107/2) − 29)/40) x 2

= 6.5 + ((53.5 − 29)/40) x 2

= 6.5 + (24.5/40) x 2

= 6.5 + 1.225

∴
 median age = 7.725

question: If a car runs at a constant speed and takes 4.5 hrs to run a distance of 225 km, what time will it take to run 150 km?
solution:
Let T be the time taken and D is the distance

For the case given in the problem, the distance covered is directly proportional to the time taken i.e D α T

⇒ D = ST where S is the constant speed

D = 225 km and T = 4.5 hrs

∴ 225 = S x 4.5

S = 225/4.5 = 50 km/hr

When D = 150 km

D = ST

150 = 50 × T

T = 150/50 = 3 hrs

So it will take 3 hrs

question: Differentiate the function y = <sup>3</sup>√x<sup>2</sup>(2x − x<sup>2</sup>)
solution:
y = <sup>3</sup>√x<sup>2</sup>(2x−x<sup>2</sup>) = x<sup>(2/3)</sup>(2x − x<sup>2</sup>)
= 2x<sup>(5/3)</sup> − x<sup>(8/3)</sup> Now, we can differentiate the function

∴dy/dx = (10x<sup>(2/3)</sup>)/3 − (8x<sup>(5/3)</sup>/3
